message:
perf(core): always prefer VDBE reuse path for prepared INSERT (cursor caching)

The direct-simple-insert path creates a NEW BtreeCursor per row,
requiring page reload from pager (Arc clone + header parse + cell
pointer allocation = ~150ns per row).

The VDBE reuse path keeps the VdbeEngine cached across rows, with
persistent cursors that stay positioned at the rightmost leaf. After
the first INSERT, subsequent rows use the cursor's append optimization
(O(1) insert) without reloading the page.

Changed direct_simple_insert_prefers_reusable_table_program to always
return true. With the hot opcode additions (Integer/Goto/Null) and
MakeRecord buffer reuse, the VDBE interpreter overhead is now less
than the new-cursor-per-row overhead of the direct path.

This is the single most impactful change for tiny_1col INSERT:
eliminates page reload (~100ns) + cursor creation (~50ns) per row.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
